rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) The beneficiary was given notice at 324 Catalpa Street, Unit 104, San Mateo, but her address was listed in the petition as 257 Vista Grande Ave, Daly City. 2) Mutual funds were purchased without prior court approval as required. Petitioner alleges that the securities within the mutual funds were securities listed on an established stock exchange and, therefore, the mutual funds themselves did not require prior court approval. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDUARDA O. BUTAC
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues (see tentative rulings for 02/26/2024, 03/18/2024, 04/10/2024 & 04/24/2024 hearings) remain unaddressed: 1) An original small estate affidavit (13100 declaration) signed by all the successors of Azuceno Olorvida must be filed with a certified copy of his death certificate. The affidavit must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). LRSF 14.59(C)(2). 2) The statement of the Bank of America account must cover periods through and including 06/30/2023, the ending date of the account. 3) Are the services performed by California Inheritance Group ordinary services that should have been performed by the administrator? If so, should CIG's 10% fee paid from the estate be deducted from the administrator's statutory compensation? 4) The schedule of proposed distribution must include a specific amount of net cash distributable to each distributee, assignee, and alternate distributee required to be identified by Probate Code section 11603(c). The proposed order must include the same descriptions. 5) A review of a copy of the closing escrow statement attached to the supplement filed on 03/05/2024 shows the total costs of $76,734.85, but Schedule E attached to the petition shows $77.535.21. 6) There is only one witness to the powers of attorney purportedly signed by Edlen Alayon. Direct notice must be given to him or her if the power of attorney is invalid. 7) There are no signatures of the witnesses to the power of attorney purportedly signed by Victor Beltran. Direct notice must be given to him if the power of attorney is invalid. 8) The power of attorney purportedly signed by Arnold Olorvida with his fingerprint was not dated. Direct notice must be given to him if the power of attorney is invalid. 9) There are no assignments signed by Salome Olorvida, Cristy Amoro, Allan A.S. Olorvida, Daniel Olorvida, Gliza Olorvida, Franz Olorvida, Artemio loysaynon, Agapito Losaynon, and Artemio Losaynon. Note: No reserve for closing expenses has been requested, although the Chase account statement shows payment of the bond premium after the account period. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY NGAI YING CHEW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/15/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. This allegation in the "supplemental" e-filed on 5/10/24 makes no sense: "Nancy Chew, acting as both executor and administrator of Frank Chew's estate, received this distribution." The order for final distribution of Frank Chew's estate ordered distribution to be made to Ming Yu Wu as executor of the estate [sic] of Nancy Ngai Ying Chew. 2. WHO paid the $96,243 to the US Treasury and $21,818 to the FTB (out of which estate or trust)? Please file proof of such bills and payments to each entity. 3. If the remaining funds were subsequently distributed to this estate, why do the two I&As total just $165,724.53? 4. "The executor of our estate waived an accounting, as is the Trustee" is a nonsensical sentence. 5. The first petition filed in this case appears to have been signed by Ming Yu Wu and filed on 6/23/20. 6. Please file the documents supporting how Ming Yu Wu is now the (successor) trustee of the Nancy Ngai Ying Chew Trust. 7. The verification of this "supplemental" refers to the "Amendment to Petition to the First and Final Report, and Petition." 8. If James Chew was the sole beneficiary of this decedent's trust, and he is now deceased, and Executor is the successor trustee of James Chew's revocable trust, who are the beneficiaries of James Chew's trust? They must be identified and must receive notice. 9. Defective notice cannot be cured by a statement in a "supplemental" to a petition. Deadline is 7/3/24 for filing responsive papers and providing courtesy copies to the assigned examinerâ¦ NOT the day before 7/15/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The nonsensical Waiver of Accounting and Waiver of Notice will be disregarded, unless Executor files proof of his appointment as Executor of James Chew's will and all persons interested in James Chew's estate or trust have received notice. The register of actions does not indicate an estate open for James Chew. Executor of this decedent's will cannot waive his own accounting or notice.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KAR WAI LEUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/17/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Another amended petition was e-filed on 5/10/24 but it was misleadingly captioned "Amendment to Frist [sic] Amended First and Final Report of Administrator," etc. In any event, an amending pleading requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] (2) There was already an amended petition filed, so if this really had been just an amendment, it should have been an amendment to the amended petition. It appears to be a fully amended petition, so it should have been captioned SECOND AMENDED PETITION, etc. (3) There were 1,265.4744 shares of Huntington Bancshs inventoried. If 100 shares were sold, why are there only 265 shares on hand? (4) There were 2,636.301 shares of Schwab Total Stock MKT Index inventoried, not 2,636.3301. (5) After Twitter was delisted from the NYSE, how much did the estate receive in exchange? An amendment to the pleading filed on 5/8/24 ("Amendment to Frist Amended First and Final Report," etc.) must be filed to address the accounting issues above. Proof of service of notice of the pleading filed on 5/8/24 and the amendment to that pleading, after the amendment is e-filed, must be filed, or waivers of notice must be filed. Deadline for providing courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues is 7/8/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Market values should NOT be part of the proposed distribution; only SHARES of securities are what the Court will order to be distributed. This was the second amended petition, but the administrator still uses the assets on hand as of 4/30/23 and 3/31/23... over a year ago. The attorney's fee declaration still does not fully meet the requirements of CRC 7.702 and the attached time sheets also include many ordinary services or services that should have been done by a paralegal or a secretary; however, in light of the attorney's voluntary reduction and lack of objections so far, the incomplete fee declaration will be accepted as filed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLADYS G. GOSZULAK
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. a) The reasons for the delay in estate administration appear to be the same as a year ago. The statutory time for closing an estate is one year when no federal estate tax is required. [Probate Code Section 12200(a).] b) This Court also requires a first accounting by a fiduciary to be for no more than two years. Executor's first accounting for the period 8/28/21 - 8/31/23 is already delinquent. c) Executor claims that she has selected a real estate broker but does not identify the person. d) It is unclear what "complicated trust administration" Executor has had to resolve. e) Executor claims that her attorney overlooked recording the change in ownership statement, although her verified I&A clearly indicated that the PCOR had been filed with the county recorder or assessor. f) Executor alleged in a "second supplemental declaration" filed on 7/18/23 that the I&A "incorrectly lists Joan Bello as trustee" of the numerous securities held in "Raymond James Goszulak Bypass Trust Joan H Bello Account #1528W292", but no Corrected I&A has been filed yet to remedy this. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLADYS G. GOSZULAK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Direct Executrix To Sell Real Property, Or Alternatively To Remove Executrix
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. a) No proposed order had been lodged or submitted at the time of this review. b) Dolores Tollestrup had not also signed the petition, although she was shown as a petitioner. [CRC 7.103(a).] c) Nothing has been filed to confirm who the successor trustee of Joan Bello's trust is. d) An estate might have to be opened for Joan Bello here if her trust did not include assets from this estate and her share of this estate will be more than $166,250, the threshold amount for a small estate in 2021, the year she died. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARCUS MCCLURE 2024 SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court is to determine whether to require evidence of legal capacity of Marcus McClure, the beneficiary of the proposed special needs trust, before the Court decides to appoint (or decides not to appoint) a guardian ad litem for Marcus. The Court cannot appoint a guardian ad litem for Marcus, unless he qualifies as a person who lacks legal capacity to make decisions under section 1003(a)(2) and within the meaning of section 812. If Marcus has the capacity within the meaning of section 812, despite his disability qualifying him for government benefits, section 3613 prohibits the Court from ordering the establishment of the proposed special needs trust without the express consent of Marcus. It should be noted that the pending ex parte petition to appoint another attorney as guardian ad litem for Marcus has not complied with LRSF 14.11(F). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing Status hearing: 6/23/2025. The administrator must file either petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 6/23/2025 if no petition has been filed. (NB: A printout of the instructions for how to appear remotely must be ATTACHED TO the notice of hearing, not served separately. Also, no notice is ordinarily required for a supplement. [Probate Code Section 1205 and CRC 7.53(b).)
